message:
fix(safety): argument injection guards for grep/find, AWS key blocking, promise bridge error handling

src/tools.rs:
- Insert "--" argument separator before user-supplied pattern in both
  GrepTool and FindTool execute() methods, preventing patterns that
  start with "-" from being interpreted as ripgrep/find flags (e.g.,
  a pattern like "--exec=rm" would otherwise be treated as a flag)
- Simplify fuzzy match normalization: remove the standalone
  normalize_for_fuzzy_match_text() and map_normalized_range_to_original()
  functions (133 lines of complex bidirectional index mapping) in favor
  of delegating normalize_for_fuzzy_match_text() directly to
  build_normalized_content(). The old mapping code was unreachable after
  the fuzzy_find_text refactor that computes replacements on normalized
  content directly.
- Refactor build_normalized_content() to use peekable iterator instead
  of pre-collecting all lines into a Vec, avoiding an O(N) allocation

src/extensions_js.rs:
- Block AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID in BLOCKED_EXACT and add _KEY_ID to
  BLOCKED_SUFFIXES to prevent AWS credential leakage to extensions
- Wrap json_to_js() conversion in match block with proper error handling:
  on conversion failure, log a warning and reject the promise with a
  structured {code: "CONVERSION_ERROR", message: ...} object instead of
  propagating an unhandled error that would leave the promise dangling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
